subtitle: The Gentle Hand Behind the Tiger Throne

panels.shot: majestic three-quarter low-angle of a royal woman standing between a temple vimana and a hospital hall
panels.action: Kundavai Pirati stands calm and tall, one hand resting on a stone pillar carved with lotus motifs and the other extended toward a line of common folk, with the towering silhouette of the Thanjavur temple rising behind her under a warm dusk sky.

dialogue.text: In the age of Rajaraja Chola, Kundavai Pirati wove power into temples, healing houses and hope.

i18n.hi: राजराज चोल के युग में कुन्दवै पिराटी ने शक्ति को मंदिरों, औषधालयों और आशा में पिरो दिया।

panels.shot: wide establishing shot from slightly elevated angle
panels.action: A panoramic view of late 10th-century Thanjavur and Pazhaiyarai shows bustling streets, granaries, canals and shrines, while in the foreground young Kundavai Pirati, in simple but fine silk, watches from a palace balcony shaded by banana leaves.

dialogue.text: In the rich Kaveri valley, where canals shone like silver threads, the Chola war-banner rose over crowded markets and ancient shrines.

i18n.hi: समृद्ध कावेरी घाटी में, जहाँ नहरें चाँदी की डोर-सी चमकती थीं, चोल ध्वज भरे बाज़ारों और प्राचीन देवालयों पर लहर रहा था।

panels.action: Young Kundavai Pirati sits on the cool stone floor of an inner hall, listening intently to an elderly brahmin teacher tracing letters and numbers on a palm leaf, while a small oil lamp flickers beside them.

dialogue.text: Born to princes and kings, Kundavai grew in halls of stone and sound, taught scriptures, accounts and the duties of rule.

i18n.hi: राजकुमारों और राजाओं के घर जन्मी कुन्दवै पत्थर और गूँज से भरे महलों में बड़ी हुई, शास्त्र, हिशाब और राज्यधर्म सीखती हुई।

panels.action: In a shaded palace courtyard, Kundavai Pirati gently but firmly questions a palace steward holding an account palm-leaf, while behind them servants distribute grain to a line of villagers.

dialogue.text: While princes trained with spear and horse, the princess studied granaries and gifts, learning how gold moved, how hunger rose and fell.

i18n.hi: जब राजकुमार भाला और घोड़े के संग अभ्यास करते, राजकुमारी कोठारों और दानों का हिसाब सीखती, कहाँ सोना बहता है, कहाँ भूख उठती-गिरती है।

panels.action: Kundavai Pirati stands beside a seated Rajaraja Chola in an audience hall, but he is rendered only as a partially visible male royal figure from behind, while ministers and landlords bow before both, their eyes respectfully lifted to her.

dialogue.text: As her younger brother Arulmolivarman rose as Rajaraja, inscriptions would name her Pirati, honoured above queens in the Chola courts.

i18n.hi: जब उसका छोटा भाई अरुल्मोलिवर्मन राजराज बना, शिलालेखों ने उसे पिराटी कहा, चोल दरबार में रानियों से ऊपर मान दिया।

panels.action: Kundavai Pirati stands under the carved stone eaves of a village shrine, hands raised in blessing as women present pots of grain and oil, while scribes sit nearby inscribing the details of her temple endowment on fresh stone.

dialogue.text: Her name spread in stone. In temple after temple, records spoke of Kundavai’s lands, jewels and tax-shares, set aside for lamp, flower and food.

i18n.hi: उसका नाम पत्थरों पर फैल गया। एक के बाद एक मंदिर में शिलालेखों ने कुन्दवै की जमीनें, गहने और कर-हिस्से गिनाए, जो दीप, फूल और अन्न को समर्पित थे।

panels.action: Inside a spacious temple hall, rows of priests, singers, and temple women stand as officials read aloud a new copperplate, while Kundavai Pirati listens, calm and composed, her gaze lingering on the faces of the gathered artisans.

dialogue.text: These gifts were not mere piety. They fed musicians, dancers, guards and cooks, binding town and temple into one living body.

i18n.hi: ये दान सिर्फ भक्ति नहीं थे। इन्होंने गायक, नर्तक, प्रहरी और रसोइए खिलाए, नगर और मंदिर को एक जीवित देह की तरह जोड़ दिया।

panels.shot: medium shot with depth of field
panels.action: In a busy temple courtyard, Kundavai Pirati steps aside from a ritual procession to speak with a blind man and his daughter, her hand lightly touching his shoulder as temple clerks watch, their palm-leaves ready.

dialogue.text: “The gods need lamps,” she murmurs, “but people need more.”

i18n.hi: “देवताओं को दीप चाहिए,” वह बुदबुदाती है, “पर लोगों को इससे भी ज़्यादा।”

panels.action: A newly built atula-salai hospital complex stands beside a temple tank: long pillared halls, clean courtyards, herbs hanging in bundles; in the foreground Kundavai Pirati gestures toward the buildings while officials and physicians stand attentive.

dialogue.text: So the inscriptions speak of an atula-salai, a hospital endowed by Kundavai, raised beside sacred waters.

i18n.hi: इसीलिए शिलालेखों में अतुल-सलाई का ज़िक्र है, कुन्दवै की बनाई औषधशाला, जो पवित्र जल के किनारे उठी।

panels.action: Inside the hospital hall, physicians grind medicines, attendants fan patients lying on woven cots, and a midwife soothes a woman in labor; Kundavai Pirati walks between the rows, her face intent and compassionate.

dialogue.text: Here, the sick lay on mats, the poor and pregnant found shelter, and the state’s wealth turned into cool hands and bitter cures.

i18n.hi: यहाँ बीमार चटाइयों पर लेटे, गरीब और गर्भवती आसरा पाते, और राज्य का धन ठंडी हथेलियों और कड़वी औषधियों में बदल जाता।

panels.shot: close-up on face in three-quarter profile
panels.action: Kundavai Pirati’s face fills the frame, lit softly from one side by a lamp; her eyes show quiet resolve as in the blurred background two scribes kneel, awaiting her decision on a land grant.

dialogue.text: “War passes,” she thinks, “but these stones will speak.”

i18n.hi: “युद्ध ढल जाता है,” वह सोचती है, “पर ये पत्थर बोलते रहेंगे।”

panels.shot: medium shot, interior with musicians in background
panels.action: In a polished-pillared hall, musicians rehearse on veena and flute, and a group of young dancers practice precise poses; Kundavai Pirati sits on a low platform, watching with a small approving smile, while a temple superintendent stands nearby.

dialogue.text: Her grants named singers, dancers, drum-beaters and readers, securing the pulse of art within the stone ribs of the temples.

i18n.hi: उसके अनुदानों में गायक, नर्तक, मृदंगिये और पाठक तक के नाम लिखे गए, ताकि कला की धड़कन मंदिरों की पत्थर-देह में चलती रहे।

panels.shot: wide hall shot from behind her shoulder
panels.action: From behind Kundavai Pirati’s shoulder we see a group of local chiefs and scribes seated on the floor with their palm-leaf bundles, as she points to a map-like floor diagram showing villages, canals and temple sites.

dialogue.text: She guided gifts like water, choosing which village, which canal, which shrine, so that each grant tied the countryside tighter to the crown.

i18n.hi: वह दान को जल-सा बहाती, कौन सा गाँव, कौन सी नहर, कौन सा देवालय चुने, ताकि हर अनुदान से देहात और राज और कसकर जुड़ें।

panels.shot: medium shot in a quieter inner chamber
panels.action: An older Kundavai Pirati sits with a young noblewoman before her, possibly a daughter-in-law or relative, placing a ring in the younger woman’s palm as they sit beside stacked copperplates and palm-leaf bundles.

dialogue.text: “Remember,” she says softly, “charity must have order.”

i18n.hi: “याद रखो,” वह धीमे कहती है, “दान में भी अनुशासन होना चाहिए।”

panels.action: In a dim palace corridor, Kundavai Pirati walks slowly past reliefs of tiger emblems and carved victories, her hand gently touching the stone, while outside the window torches flicker in the night.

dialogue.text: She saw campaigns march out and triumphal processions return, but her work lay in what remained when drums fell silent.

i18n.hi: वह अभियानों को कूच करते और विजय-जुलूसों को लौटते देखती रही, पर उसका काम उन चीज़ों में था जो नगाड़े थमने के बाद भी बची रहती थीं।

panels.shot: high-angle view of an inscription being carved
panels.action: A stonecutter chisels fine Grantha and Tamil letters into a temple wall, while Kundavai Pirati stands a little distance away, hands folded, as an official reads aloud the words naming her endowment and the conditions attached.

dialogue.text: On walls of Rajaraja’s temples and in lesser shrines, the records fixed her choices, setting rules for how each coin must serve.

i18n.hi: राजराज के विशाल मंदिरों से लेकर छोटे देवालयों तक की दीवारों पर शिलालेखों ने उसके फैसले बाँध दिए, हर सिक्के की सेवा तय कर दी।

panels.shot: time-lapse style wide shot concept, static image showing different eras
panels.action: The same temple complex is seen under different touches of light: in the foreground Kundavai Pirati stands in her own age, while behind her, ghostlike, later generations of devotees, physicians and artists move through the same stone corridors.

dialogue.text: Centuries would pass. Kingdoms would shift. Yet lamps still burned, voices still rose, and healers worked in the houses she endowed.

i18n.hi: सदियाँ गुजर गईं। राज बदले। फिर भी दीप जलते रहे, स्वर उठते रहे, और वैद्य उन औषधशालाओं में काम करते रहे जिन्हें उसने बसाया था।

panels.action: Kundavai Pirati stands at the edge of a temple tank at dusk, her reflection mixing with the reflection of the towering vimana and the simple cottages beyond, as a faint breeze ripples the water.

dialogue.text: Thus history remembers Kundavai not by battles, but by temples lit, arts nourished and the quiet reach of a princess who turned power toward care.

i18n.hi: इस तरह इतिहास कुन्दवै को युद्धों से नहीं, बल्कि प्रज्वलित मंदिरों, पाली हुई कलाओं और उस नर्म हाथ से याद रखता है जिसने सत्ता को सेवा की ओर मोड़ दिया।

subtitle_i18n.en: The Gentle Hand Behind the Tiger Throne
